_schema.purpose: Per-locality fluid-chemistry data referenced by scenario_* functions in vugg.py and the JS mirror. Populated incrementally by the SCENARIO-CHEMISTRY-AUDIT task (see proposals/TASK-BRIEF-SCENARIO-CHEMISTRY-AUDIT.md). Each entry cites the published source for every value; gaps are left null and explicitly noted in notes[].

units.fluid.*: ppm unless otherwise noted (same abstraction scale as FluidChemistry in vugg.py — sim values are scaled, not raw brine analyses)
units.depth_km: kilometers of burial at time of fluid event

herkimer_middleville.geological_setting: Vugs in dolomitic host. Hydrocarbon-mediated silica liberation from pressure solution during Alleghenian burial to >3 km; silica-saturated basinal brine precipitated double-terminated quartz in isolated pockets. Classic clear-quartz-in-vug locality.

temperature_C.source: Alleghenian-age peak burial T from conodont alteration indices and vitrinite reflectance in the Appalachian basin carbonates at this depth; see Harris et al. 1978 (USGS Prof. Paper 1197) and Friedman & Sanders 1982 for Herkimer-specific discussion. Fluid-inclusion homogenization temperatures for authigenic quartz in the Appalachian Cambrian-Ordovician section fall in this range.

pressure_kbar.source: ~3-4 km burial at time of quartz event (Selleck 1978 for Mohawk Valley stratigraphy + lithostatic pressure calc).

SiO2.note: Elevated over quartz equilibrium (~220 ppm at 180 C per Rimstidt 1997) — hydrocarbon-cracking + pressure solution supply. Sim-scale abstraction, not raw ppm.

Ca.note: Lower than a typical Appalachian basin brine (Hanor 1994 compendium: 5000-20000 ppm raw), because most Ca is sequestered in surrounding dolomite cement — the pocket is the silica-overpressured residue. Sim-scale abstraction.

CO3.note: Most carbonate has migrated out as dolomite cement; pocket residual only.

Mg.note: Dolomitic-aquifer signature, Mg/Ca ~0.6 — matches audit brief's 'hydrothermal' range (50-500 ppm). Brief-required non-zero Mg.

Fe.note: Trace from dolomite host (wall_Fe_ppm ~2000 in host rock, but solution-phase is low because reducing and mildly acid-buffered).

Ti.note: Controls TitaniQ thermometer signal in the precipitated quartz (Wark & Watson 2006).

salinity_wt_pct_NaCleq.note: Appalachian basin burial brines 15-25 wt% NaCl-eq (Hanor 1994); fluid inclusions in Herkimer quartz also commonly saline (petroleum + brine two-phase).

O2.note: Reducing — hydrocarbon coexists (solid anthraxolite + liquid petroleum inclusions are diagnostic of Herkimer).

wall.source: Little Falls Formation is a dolostone; host-rock wall_Mg_ppm should be elevated (the VugWall default wall_Mg_ppm=1000 is already tagged 'higher if dolomite').

references: Friedman, G.M. & Sanders, J.E. (1982). Time-temperature-burial significance of Devonian anthracite implies former great (~6.5 km) depth of burial of Catskill Mountains, New York. Geology 10(2): 93-96. [Regional burial thermal history]
Hanor, J.S. (1994). Origin of saline fluids in sedimentary basins. Geol. Soc. London Spec. Pub. 78: 151-174. [Basinal-brine compositional compendium — Na/Ca/Mg/K ratios and salinity ranges]

Harris, A.G., Harris, L.D., & Epstein, J.B. (1978). Oil and gas data from Paleozoic rocks in the Appalachian basin. USGS Prof. Paper 1197. [Conodont alteration index constraints on peak T for the Cambrian-Ordovician section]
Rimstidt, J.D. (1997). Quartz solubility at low temperatures. Geochim. Cosmochim. Acta 61(13): 2553-2558. [Quartz solubility equation used to justify SiO2=500 as supersaturated at 180 C]
Selleck, B.W. (1978). Syndepositional brecciation in the Little Falls Dolostone of central New York. J. Sed. Petrol. 48: 1135-1142. [Host stratigraphy and burial depth]
Wark, D.A. & Watson, E.B. (2006). TitaniQ: a titanium-in-quartz geothermometer. Contrib. Mineral. Petrol. 152: 743-754. [Ti-in-quartz thermometer used in sim]
data_gaps: No modern peer-reviewed microthermometry study with both Th and Tm_ice specifically for Herkimer pocket quartz appears to be widely indexed; the 140-200 C window is inferred from regional Alleghenian thermal maturity, not directly measured on Herkimer quartz. A dedicated FI study (the type done for Joplin brines in Roedder 1976) remains a gap.
LA-ICP-MS of Herkimer fluid inclusions is not in the open literature; basinal-brine ratios are imported from Hanor 1994 Appalachian-basin averages.

intentionally_zero.Au: Burial-diagenetic quartz at 140-200 C in basinal brine; no Au geochemistry. The Mohawk Valley Cambrian-Ordovician section is unrelated to gold deposits. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

arsenopyrite.reason: No As in fluid (cooling/silica scenario). Herkimer dolostone host has no documented arsenopyrite occurrence.

scorodite.reason: No As in fluid; reducing dolostone diagenesis (O2=0.1) is wrong Eh regime anyway.

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo in fluid; no porphyry-Mo signature in this clean Cambrian dolostone.

herkimer_middleville.audit_pass: | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

barite.reason: No Ba in fluid; Cambrian dolostone diagenesis is reducing (O2=0.1) anyway.

jarosite.reason: No K + reducing fluid; pH=6.8 outside acid window.

alunite.reason: No K + Al + S sulfate; reducing dolostone diagenesis is wrong Eh regime.

antlerite.reason: No Cu; pH=6.8 way too high anyway.

anhydrite.reason: No S in fluid (Cambrian dolostone is sulfate-poor); T=180 would otherwise be in range.

bingham_canyon.display_name: Bingham Canyon Cu-Mo-Au porphyry, Oquirrh Mountains, UT
bingham_canyon.host_formation: Late-Eocene (~38 Ma) quartz-monzonite-porphyry intruding Pennsylvanian-Permian sediments
bingham_canyon.alteration_zoning: potassic core, phyllic shell, propylitic rim
bingham_canyon.fluid_event_summary: Magmatic-hydrothermal evolution from deep-center brine (~7 wt% NaCl-eq, subequal Na/K/Fe/Cu) to upper-level brine endmember (~45 wt% NaCl-eq) coexisting with low-density vapor (~0.2 g/cm3). Discrete pulses: early Cu, late Mo (Seo et al. 2012), epithermal Au-Te-Ag cap. Vug-stage fluid is interpreted as the cooled, vapor-condensate-mixed residue of the brine plume.
bingham_canyon.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Cl, Mg, Ag, Te to populate the brine-element baseline. Drift fixes between Python and JS scenario_porphyry: Pb=15 mirrored from Python to JS, Na/K/Al mirrored from JS to Python, event_molybdenum_pulse mirrored from Python to JS, event_copper_injection Pb increment mirrored from Python to JS. Au=2 added later when the Au field + grow_native_gold engine landed (previously held in pending_schema_additions). Existing chemistry preserved (no retuning). | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

Cl.note: NaCl-KCl brine anion, paired with existing Na+K. salinity=10 wt% NaCl-eq remains the bulk indicator [Landtwing et al. 2010].

Mg.note: Wallrock interaction; Bingham brine fluid Mg 50-200 ppm raw [Landtwing et al. 2010]; sim-scale conservative. Brief-required non-zero Mg.

Ag.note: Argentiferous-Cu signature; brine-stage fluid Ag 1-50 ppm raw in published porphyry-fluid LA-ICP-MS [Heinrich 2007].

Te.note: Epithermal-cap telluride trace (calaverite, sylvanite at Bingham upper levels).

Au.note: Porphyry-Cu-Au signature — Bingham produced ~200 t Au cumulatively. ~1 ppm Au in fluid inclusions per Landtwing et al. 2010 LA-ICP-MS; sim-scale 2 matches Te=2 trace abstraction. Activated when grow_native_gold engine landed.

preserved_intentional_values.Cu_note: Cu=0 at init by design; delivered in pulses by event_copper_injection at steps 25 and 60.
preserved_intentional_values.Mo_note: Mo=0 at init by design; delivered by event_molybdenum_pulse at step 45 (Seo et al. 2012).

references: Landtwing, M.R., Furrer, C., Redmond, P.B., Pettke, T., Guillong, M., Heinrich, C.A. (2010). The Bingham Canyon Porphyry Cu-Mo-Au Deposit. III. Zoned Copper-Gold Ore Deposition by Magmatic Vapor Expansion. Economic Geology 105(1): 91-118. [Primary LA-ICP-MS fluid-inclusion source for Bingham brine compositions]

Seo, J.H., Guillong, M., Heinrich, C.A. (2012). The Role of Sulfur in the Formation of Magmatic-Hydrothermal Copper-Gold Deposits. Earth Planet. Sci. Lett. 282: 323-328. [Mo timing decoupled from Cu — basis for event_molybdenum_pulse]
Heinrich, C.A. (2007). Fluid-fluid interactions in magmatic-hydrothermal ore formation. Rev. Mineral. Geochem. 65: 363-387. [Porphyry-fluid Ag/Te/Sb/Bi range]

Kouzmanov, K. & Pokrovski, G.S. (2012). Hydrothermal controls on metal distribution in porphyry Cu (-Mo-Au) systems. SEG Spec. Pub. 16: 573-618. [As activity in epithermal Cu, Sb-As-Bi greisen signature]

Klemm, L.M., Pettke, T., Heinrich, C.A. (2007). Hydrothermal evolution of the El Teniente deposit, Chile: porphyry Cu-Mo ore deposition from low-salinity magmatic fluids. Econ. Geol. 102(6): 1021-1045. [Comparison brine compositions]
data_gaps: Te trace is asserted from epithermal-cap evidence; direct Te quantification from Bingham core fluid inclusions is not widely published.

Au=2 is now active in the scenario (grow_native_gold engine landed). Calaverite/sylvanite (Au-Te tellurides) remain in pending — their engines aren't implemented yet, so the existing Au+Te coexistence in Bingham init only produces native_gold currently, not the Au-Te species that real Bingham upper levels host.

pending_schema_additions._purpose: Values pre-researched and ready to plug in once the corresponding mineral engine exists. Au itself is now active.

calaverite_sylvanite.blockers: Au+Te coexistence in Bingham init (Au=2, Te=2) would naturally produce Au-Te telluride species (calaverite AuTe2, sylvanite (Au,Ag)Te2) in real Bingham upper levels, but grow_calaverite / grow_sylvanite engines are not implemented yet. When they land, no fluid-chemistry change needed; the Au+Te+Ag chemistry is already present.
calaverite_sylvanite.rationale: Bingham upper-level epithermal cap hosts these tellurides per Landtwing 2010. Currently the sim deposits all the Au as native_gold; adding Au-Te competition in grow_native_gold would partition some Au into telluride growth instead.
calaverite_sylvanite.source: Landtwing et al. 2010 + Cook et al. 2009 (Au-Ag-Te systematics in porphyry caps).

arsenopyrite.reason: T=400 C, As=15, Fe=30, S=60, O2=0.2, pH=4.5 -> sigma=2.18 at init. Will nucleate in early reducing high-T phase before pyrite/chalcopyrite consume Fe+S. Will trap fluid Au (init Au=2 ppm) as invisible-gold trace_Au.
arsenopyrite.real_locality_evidence: Bingham polymetallic porphyry mineralization includes arsenopyrite per Heinrich 2007 and Kouzmanov & Pokrovski 2012 (As activity in epithermal Cu systems). Tetrahedrite/tennantite fahlores (already implemented) are the As-bearing endpoints; arsenopyrite is the higher-T precursor.

scorodite.reason: Will nucleate after step 85 event_oxidation raises O2 above 0.5. pH=4.5 is in stability window. Substrate-prefers any dissolving arsenopyrite from the early phase — captures the supergene Fe + AsO4 release.
scorodite.real_locality_evidence: Bingham oxidized cap contains scorodite associated with arsenopyrite oxidation per Lindgren 1924 + later Bingham mineral inventories.

ferrimolybdite.reason: Mo arrives via event_molybdenum_pulse (step 45). After event_oxidation (step 85), Mo + Fe + O2 all present. Competes with wulfenite for MoO4 (Bingham has Pb=15 too); ferrimolybdite nucleates faster (sigma threshold 1.0 vs wulfenite 1.2) so wins early window.
ferrimolybdite.real_locality_evidence: Ferrimolybdite documented at Bingham oxidation cap (Climax-Bingham comparable porphyry-Mo geology); under-represented in collection lists because powdery yellow fuzz vs collector-prized wulfenite.

barite.reason: Ba=0 in init (porphyry brine is not Ba-rich); no Ba-delivering event.

jarosite.reason: K=40, Fe=30, S=60 all sufficient. Init pH=4.5 borderline (gate is 5); event_oxidation (step 85) raises O2 to oxidizing range. Once pH drops below 4 from H2SO4 production, jarosite fires.
jarosite.event_dependency: event_oxidation (step 85) for O2; pH drop must follow from sulfide oxidation

alunite.reason: K=40, Al=15, S=60 all sufficient — the textbook porphyry advanced argillic alteration cap. Same event dependency as jarosite (needs O2 + acid).
alunite.event_dependency: event_oxidation (step 85) + acid pH from H2SO4 production

brochantite.reason: Cu pulse at step 25 + 60 produces Cu, but it's consumed by chalcopyrite/bornite/chalcocite formation in the reducing primary phase. Post-oxidation Cu sulfide oxidation could yield brochantite but the supergene Cu pool is small.

antlerite.reason: Same as brochantite — no Cu sulfate niche in Bingham scenario.

anhydrite.reason: T=400 init perfect for high-T anhydrite, BUT init O2=0.2 is below the 0.3 gate. Fires after event_oxidation raises O2. Bingham porphyry deep-zone anhydrite + chalcopyrite paragenesis (Roedder 1971) is the geological reference.

tri_state.display_name: Tri-State Pb-Zn-Ag district (Joplin / Picher / Galena, MO-KS-OK)
tri_state.host_formation: Mississippian Boone Formation cherty limestone (and Reeds Spring / St. Joe formations locally)
tri_state.fluid_event_summary: Late Pennsylvanian-Early Permian basinal brine expulsion; NaCl-CaCl2 brines, 18-24 wt% NaCl-eq, 90-150 C ore stage. Stoffell et al. 2008 LA-ICP-MS shows Tri-State and Northern Arkansas brines were chemically distinct from Viburnum Trend, with Tri-State having higher Pb-Zn-Ag and lower Ba relative to the Viburnum brines. Ore deposition by sulfide saturation when metal-bearing brine met sulfur-bearing groundwater.
tri_state.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Na, K, Cl (NaCl-CaCl2 brine baseline), Ag (argentiferous-galena), Ba (local barite gangue), Sr (basinal-brine tracer), Cu (trace chalcopyrite). Drift-fix: Pb=40 mirrored from JS init into Python init. Existing chemistry preserved (no retuning). | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite. | v5-gap-fill (Apr 2026) — O2 bumped 0.0 → 0.25 to match real MVT mildly-reducing chemistry at the SO₄/H₂S boundary. Activates dormant Ba/Sr pools for barite + celestine. Plus supersat O2 saturation retuned to /0.4 (matches the SO₄/H₂S boundary).

Na.note: NaCl-CaCl2 basinal brine; raw 50-80,000 ppm [Stoffell 2008]; sim-scale abstraction.

Cl.note: Brine anion paired with Na+K. Tri-State raw fluid 120-220,000 ppm Cl. salinity=15 wt% NaCl-eq remains bulk indicator; free Cl enables pyromorphite/chlorargyrite.

Ag.note: Argentiferous-galena signature. Tri-State galena 50-300 ppm Ag in mineral; brine Ag much lower. Historical Joplin smelters recovered Ag as Pb byproduct.

Ba.note: Local barite gangue at Tri-State (less than Viburnum Trend, which is the high-Ba MVT endmember per Stoffell 2008).

Sr.note: Basinal-brine tracer (Hanor 1994); minor celestine documented at Tri-State.

Cu.note: Trace chalcopyrite documented at Tri-State even though Cu is not a primary commodity.

O2.note: O2 bumped 0.0 → 0.25 (v5 gap-fill, Apr 2026): real MVT brine sits at the SO₄/H₂S Eh boundary where some sulfate persists alongside H₂S — barite + galena coexistence is the diagnostic MVT assemblage. Bumping from default 0.0 to 0.25 unlocks dormant barite + celestine without destabilizing existing sulfide assemblages (sulfide gates are O2 ≤ 0.8). Plus barite + celestine supersaturation O2 saturation tuned from O2/1.0 to O2/0.4 (saturates at the SO₄/H₂S boundary, geochemically correct). Source: Anderson & Macqueen 1982 (MVT review); Stoffell et al. 2008 (Tri-State LA-ICP-MS brine analyses).

preserved_intentional_values.Zn_note: Zn=0 at init by design; delivered by event_fluid_mixing at step 20 (Zn=150).
preserved_intentional_values.S_note: S=0 at init by design; delivered by event_fluid_mixing at step 20 (S=120). The low-S initial fluid is what makes MVT 'fluid mixing' the depositional mechanism (not single-fluid cooling).

references: Roedder, E. (1976). Fluid inclusions as samples of ore-forming fluids. In: Wolf, K.H. (ed.), Handbook of Strata-bound and Stratiform Ore Deposits, vol. 2, Elsevier, p. 67-110. [Tri-State fluid-inclusion microthermometry baseline]
Ohle, E.L. (1959). Some considerations in determining the origin of ore deposits of the Mississippi Valley type. Econ. Geol. 54: 769-789. [Genetic model for MVT brine flow]
Hagni, R.D. (1976). Tri-State ore deposits: the character of their host rocks and their genesis. In: Wolf (ed.), Handbook vol. 6, p. 457-494. [Detailed Tri-State mineralogy and paragenesis]

Stoffell, B., Appold, M.S., Wilkinson, J.J., McClean, N.A., Jeffries, T.E. (2008). Geochemistry and Evolution of Mississippi Valley-Type Mineralizing Brines from the Tri-State and Northern Arkansas Districts Determined by LA-ICP-MS Microanalysis of Fluid Inclusions. Econ. Geol. 103(7): 1411-1435. [Primary LA-ICP-MS brine compositions for Tri-State]
Hanor, J.S. (1994). Origin of saline fluids in sedimentary basins. Geol. Soc. London Spec. Pub. 78: 151-174. [Basinal-brine compositional compendium]
Leach, D.L., Sangster, D.F. (1993). Mississippi Valley-type lead-zinc deposits. Geol. Assoc. Canada Spec. Paper 40: 289-314. [MVT classification + genetic synthesis]
data_gaps: Stoffell et al. 2008 PDF was not machine-extractable in this audit pass — exact LA-ICP-MS sample-by-sample numbers were not retrieved; gap-fills use the paper's reported ranges + general MVT-brine compendium data.
Ore-stage T (90-150 C) is below scenario T=180 — preserved scenario T as intentional simulation-time choice.

intentionally_zero.Au: MVT brines transport Pb-Zn-Ba-F-Ag but not Au — Au has different transport chemistry (needs higher T or Au-Cl/Au-HS complexes that the cool basinal brine doesn't sustain). Stoffell 2008 LA-ICP-MS doesn't report Au from Tri-State fluid inclusions. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

pending_schema_additions._purpose: Values pre-researched and ready to plug in once the corresponding FluidChemistry schema field exists.

Cd.rationale: Tri-State sphalerite carries Cd substituting for Zn — typically 1000-5000 ppm Cd in the mineral, raw fluid Cd ~1-10 ppm. Greenockite (CdS) is the diagnostic yellow coating on Tri-State sphalerite. Adding Cd to the schema would unlock greenockite nucleation as a discrete late-stage phase, plus enable Cd-trace tracking in sphalerite growth zones (TitaniQ-analog Cd-in-sphalerite thermometer).
Cd.source: Schwartz, M.O. (2000). Cadmium in zinc deposits: economic geology of a polluting element. Internat. Geol. Rev. 42: 445-469. Plus general Tri-State greenockite occurrence in Hagni 1976.
Cd.blockers: FluidChemistry has no Cd field. Need: Cd field in dataclass + JS class, grow_greenockite (CdS) implementation, minerals.json entry, optionally Cd-in-sphalerite trace tracking in grow_sphalerite.

arsenopyrite.reason: MVT scenario is low-T (~100-150 C) — below arsenopyrite mesothermal window. As=0 in fluid (only Sb=10 trace). Tri-State is not As-rich.

scorodite.reason: No As in fluid; pH=7.2 is alkaline (above scorodite stability anyway).

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo in MVT signature. Ozark MVT lacks porphyry-Mo affinity.

barite.reason: Ba=20, S=120 (post event_fluid_mixing step 20), O2=0.25 (v5 bump). Verified seed-42: 6 active barite crystals, max 32 µm. The barite + galena MVT-diagnostic coexistence now reproduces in the sim.
barite.audit_action: Resolved by v5 commit. O2 bump + supersat O2/0.4 retune both required.

celestine.reason: Sr=15, S=120, O2=0.25. Verified seed-42: 10 celestine crystals, max 111 µm. Minor celestine in Tri-State carbonate gangue (Hanor 1994) now reproduces.

jarosite.reason: T=180 above the 100°C supergene window; pH=7.2 above acid window.

alunite.reason: Al=3 below the 10 ppm gate; pH=7.2 too high.

brochantite.reason: Cu=3 below the 10 ppm gate (Tri-State is Pb-Zn-Ba, not Cu).

anhydrite.reason: Ca=80 (selenite-equivalent), T=180 → above selenite range but salinity=18 too low for low-T branch and high-T branch needs O2≥0.3 (Tri-State has O2=0). Could fire if both O2 and salinity bumped.

sweetwater_viburnum.display_name: Sweetwater Mine, Viburnum Trend, Reynolds County, Missouri
sweetwater_viburnum.fluid_event_summary: Late Pennsylvanian-Early Permian basinal brine expulsion driven by Ouachita orogeny dewatering. NaCl-CaCl2 brine, 18-24 wt% NaCl-eq, 90-150 C ore stage. Viburnum is the high-Ba / lower-Ag MVT endmember (Stoffell et al. 2008 LA-ICP-MS contrast with Tri-State). The 'reactive wall' mechanic models repeated acid pulses dissolving the carbonate host and triggering pH-recovery growth bursts on existing crystals — the diagnostic Viburnum Pb-Zn paragenesis (sphalerite + galena + marcasite ± barite, with dolomite-calcite gangue).
sweetwater_viburnum.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Na, K, Cl (NaCl-CaCl2 brine baseline), Ag (Viburnum galena trace, lower than Tri-State), Sr (basinal-brine tracer). No drift between Python and JS (both runtimes already consistent). Existing chemistry preserved (no retuning). | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite. | v5-gap-fill (Apr 2026) — O2 bumped 0.0 → 0.25 to match real MVT mildly-reducing chemistry at the SO₄/H₂S boundary. Activates dormant Ba/Sr pools for barite + celestine. Plus supersat O2 saturation retuned to /0.4 (matches the SO₄/H₂S boundary).

Na.note: Slightly below Tri-State Na=80 reflecting Viburnum's somewhat lower Pb-Zn-Ag tenor and different basin source [Sverjensky 1981; Stoffell 2008].

Cl.note: Brine anion paired with Na+K. salinity=18 wt% NaCl-eq remains bulk indicator; free Cl enables Pb-Cl chemistry.

Ag.note: Viburnum galena carries Ag at lower abundance than Tri-State (Stoffell 2008 LA-ICP-MS contrast). Sweetwater historically not a major Ag producer.

Sr.note: Basinal-brine tracer (Hanor 1994); minor celestine documented in Viburnum carbonate gangue.

O2.note: O2 bumped 0.0 → 0.25 (v5 gap-fill, Apr 2026): real MVT brine sits at the SO₄/H₂S Eh boundary where some sulfate persists alongside H₂S — barite + galena coexistence is the diagnostic MVT assemblage. Bumping from default 0.0 to 0.25 unlocks dormant barite + celestine without destabilizing existing sulfide assemblages (sulfide gates are O2 ≤ 0.8). Plus barite + celestine supersaturation O2 saturation tuned from O2/1.0 to O2/0.4 (saturates at the SO₄/H₂S boundary, geochemically correct). Source: Anderson & Macqueen 1982 (MVT review); Stoffell et al. 2008 (Tri-State LA-ICP-MS brine analyses).

preserved_intentional_values.Mg_note: Mg=100 sets Mg/Ca ~0.4, above dolomite gate (0.3); calcite still wins by Mg-poisoning math. Aragonite stays absent (its threshold is ~1.5).
preserved_intentional_values.Ba_note: Ba=25 reflects Viburnum's high-Ba MVT endmember status; barite is a documented late gangue.

references: Sverjensky, D.A. (1981). The origin of a Mississippi Valley-type deposit in the Viburnum Trend, southeast Missouri. Econ. Geol. 76(7): 1848-1872. [Primary Viburnum/Sweetwater geochemistry source]

Leach, D.L., Sangster, D.F., Kelley, K.D., Large, R.R., Garven, G., Allen, C.R., Gutzmer, J., Walters, S. (2010). Sediment-hosted lead-zinc deposits in Earth history. Econ. Geol. 105(3): 593-625. [MVT review with Viburnum case study]

Stoffell, B., Appold, M.S., Wilkinson, J.J., McClean, N.A., Jeffries, T.E. (2008). Geochemistry and Evolution of Mississippi Valley-Type Mineralizing Brines from the Tri-State and Northern Arkansas Districts Determined by LA-ICP-MS Microanalysis of Fluid Inclusions. Econ. Geol. 103(7): 1411-1435. [Tri-State vs Viburnum contrast: Tri-State higher Ag, Viburnum higher Ba]
Hanor, J.S. (1994). Origin of saline fluids in sedimentary basins. Geol. Soc. London Spec. Pub. 78: 151-174. [Basinal-brine compendium for Sr / minor element ratios]
data_gaps: Sweetwater-specific (vs district-wide Viburnum) fluid-inclusion LA-ICP-MS data is sparse; trace-element values are inferred from district averages.

The 'acid pulse' mechanic is an abstraction — real Viburnum mineralization is interpreted as continuous brine flow with pH evolving via wallrock buffering, not discrete acid injections. The pulse model preserves the dissolution-precipitation cycle pedagogically.

intentionally_zero.Au: Same MVT-brine context as Tri-State — basinal brines don't carry Au. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

arsenopyrite.reason: Reactive_wall is low-T MVT-cousin (T~100 C) — below mesothermal arsenopyrite window. As=0 in fluid.

scorodite.reason: No As in fluid; pH=7.0 alkaline.

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo. Sweetwater is a Pb-Zn district, no Mo affinity.

barite.reason: Ba=25, S=60+ (init + acid_pulse events deliver more), O2=0.25. Verified seed-42: 14 active barite crystals, max 63 µm. Viburnum is the high-Ba MVT endmember (Stoffell 2008) — the barite gangue assemblage now reproduces.

celestine.reason: Sr=12, S=60+, O2=0.25. Verified seed-42: 8 celestine crystals, max 56 µm.

jarosite.reason: T=140 above supergene window; pH=7.0 above acid window.

alunite.reason: Al=3 below gate; pH=7 too high.

brochantite.reason: No Cu (Sweetwater is Pb-Zn district, not Cu).

anhydrite.reason: Ca only 100 ppm or so; same O2 gate; selenite+gypsum is the appropriate phase.

warren_district.display_name: Bisbee / Warren Mining District, Cochise County, Arizona
warren_district.host_formation: Laramide (Late Cretaceous - Early Tertiary, ~70-65 Ma) quartz-monzonite porphyry intruding Paleozoic Escabrosa Limestone + Abrigo Formation
warren_district.production_history: ~8 Mt Cu, ~25 Moz Ag, ~3 Moz Au historically (1880-1975). Type-locality for the supergene Cu paragenesis cascade (chalcopyrite -> chalcocite -> cuprite -> native Cu -> azurite -> malachite -> chrysocolla).
warren_district.fluid_event_summary: Three-phase history: (1) primary Laramide magmatic-hydrothermal phase, hot reducing brine, chalcopyrite-bornite-pyrite-magnetite assemblage; (2) Mesozoic-Cenozoic uplift exposes ore to meteoric water, pyrite oxidation drops pH, Cu^2+ leaches and descends; (3) supergene cascade: chalcocite enrichment blanket -> cuprite -> native Cu -> azurite (high pCO2 monsoon) -> malachite (pCO2 drop) -> chrysocolla (silica seep). Each step corresponds to a documented Bisbee specimen type.
warren_district.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Ag (~25 Moz Ag historical production), Mg (Escabrosa Limestone host), P (enables pyromorphite given Pb+Cl already present), Sb (tetrahedrite-tennantite sulfosalt trace). No drift between Python and JS init (both already consistent). Existing chemistry preserved (no retuning); event sequence (9 events spanning 340 steps) preserved. Au=3 added later when the Au field + grow_native_gold engine landed (previously held in pending_schema_additions). | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

Ag.note: Warren District produced ~25 Moz Ag historically — argentiferous galena + tetrahedrite + argentite + minor native Ag. Higher than the prior MVT scenarios (Tri-State Ag=5, Sweetwater Ag=3) reflecting Bisbee's Ag-rich character.

Mg.note: Escabrosa Limestone host is dolomitic in places; brine Mg from carbonate dissolution. Conservative — Bisbee doesn't have prominent dolomitization.

P.note: Enables pyromorphite (Pb-Cl-PO4) given existing Pb=15 + Cl=400 + supergene oxidation events. Pyromorphite is a documented minor Bisbee supergene Pb species alongside cerussite + anglesite.

Sb.note: Tetrahedrite (Cu-Sb-S) is the documented sulfosalt at Bisbee. Mirrors the existing Bi=2 / As=8 greisen-trace triplet.

Au.note: Bisbee ~3 Moz Au historically; supergene-preserved native Au + auriferous chalcocite throughout the oxidation zone [Graeme et al. 2019]. Slightly higher than Bingham's Au=2 reflecting Bisbee's well-preserved supergene zone where Au accumulates. Activated when grow_native_gold engine landed.

preserved_intentional_values.Cu_note: Cu=400 is upper end of primary porphyry; the whole district budget concentrated in the sim relative to nature (we don't model the 10 km^3 of leached rock).
preserved_intentional_values.Cl_note: Cl=400 + salinity=30 wt% NaCl-eq reflects the hypersaline (35-55 wt%) primary brine per Graeme et al. 2019 fluid inclusion data.
preserved_intentional_values.T_note: T=400 is mid-range of Graeme et al. 2019 fluid-inclusion 320-450 C. Cooling events drop T through the supergene sequence.

references: Graeme, R.W., Graeme, R.W. III, Graeme, D.B. (2019). Bisbee, Arizona: A Mineralogical History. Mineralogical Record, vol. 50 monograph. [The modern Bisbee mineralogy + paragenesis monograph]
Bryant, D.G. (1968). Intrusive breccias associated with ore, Warren (Bisbee) Mining District, Arizona. Econ. Geol. 63(1): 1-12. [District geology + Sacramento Hill intrusion]
Crane, W.R. (1911). Geology of the Bisbee district, Arizona. USGS Prof. Paper 21. [Historical district geology baseline]
Vink, B.W. (1986). Stability relations of malachite and azurite. Mineral. Mag. 50: 41-47. [pCO2 thermodynamics of azurite-malachite-chrysocolla cascade — core mechanic]

Mote, T.I., Becker, T.A., Renne, P., Brimhall, G.H. (2001). Chronology of exotic mineralization at El Salvador, Chile, by 40Ar/39Ar dating of copper chlorides and chrysocolla. Econ. Geol. 96(2): 351-366. [Supergene chrysocolla geochemistry — applicable to Bisbee chrysocolla]
data_gaps: P=5 is a minimum-viable value to enable pyromorphite nucleation; actual Bisbee fluid P content not directly published in fluid-inclusion studies.
Sb=5 mirrors the Bi/As traces conservatively; Bisbee tetrahedrite Sb content is well-documented in mineral analyses but fluid-stage Sb is not.

pending_schema_additions._purpose: Values pre-researched and ready to plug in once the corresponding mineral engine exists. Au itself is now active.

auriferous_chalcocite_trace.blockers: Bisbee's supergene Au literature (Graeme et al. 2019) emphasizes that much of the Au is hosted as a trace within chalcocite rather than as discrete native_gold crystals. Adding an Au-trace tracker on grow_chalcocite (parallel to how Mn/Fe traces are tracked in calcite) would let the sim record this dual mode. Currently all Au in Bisbee precipitates as discrete native_gold instead of partitioning into chalcocite.
auriferous_chalcocite_trace.rationale: Faithful Bisbee paragenesis would record both native gold pockets AND ppm-Au-bearing chalcocite zones, with the latter being the more economically significant mode.

arsenopyrite.reason: T=400 C, Fe=200 (very high), As=8, S=150, O2=0.05, pH=5.0 -> sigma >12 raw at init. Massive nucleation will consume As quickly. Will trap fluid Au (init Au=3 ppm) extensively.
arsenopyrite.real_locality_evidence: Warren District arsenic from arsenopyrite is the As source that supergene-oxidation later releases as scorodite. Bisbee documented arsenopyrite per Graeme et al. 2019 + earlier Bisbee mineral inventories.

scorodite.reason: After ev_uplift_weathering (step 65) drops pH and raises O2, scorodite forms from arsenopyrite oxidation products. ev_oxidation_zone (step 145) reinforces.
scorodite.real_locality_evidence: Bisbee oxidation zone scorodite is part of the supergene mineral assemblage per Graeme et al. 2019.

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo in Warren District fluid (init Mo=0; no Mo-delivering event). Bisbee is Cu-Pb-Zn-Au, not Cu-Mo. Mineral not expected at this locality.

barite.reason: Ba=0 in Bisbee fluid; not a documented Bisbee species in significant amounts.

jarosite.reason: K=80, Fe=200 (very high), S=150 all sufficient. After ev_uplift_weathering (step 65) drops pH and raises O2, jarosite fires strongly. The yellow rim on weathered Bisbee pyrite is the diagnostic AMD signature.

alunite.reason: K=80, Al=20, S=150 all sufficient. Same trigger as jarosite. Bisbee oxidation cap may include alunite though it's less famous than the Cu suite.

brochantite.reason: Cu (delivered by primary cooling event) + S=150 + post-uplift O2 rise. Bisbee brochantite is well-documented — emerald-green prisms on the famous 'Bisbee blue azurite' assemblages.
brochantite.event_dependency: Primary cooling delivers Cu; ev_uplift_weathering provides O2

antlerite.reason: Will fire IF pH drops below 3.5 during the supergene phase. Less common than brochantite at Bisbee (which is more Cu-S-OH neutral) but possible.

anhydrite.reason: T=400 init + Ca=60 + S=150, but init O2=0.05 well below gate. Fires after supergene events raise O2 above 0.3.

ouro_preto.display_name: Ouro Preto Imperial Topaz veins, Minas Gerais, Brazil
ouro_preto.fluid_event_summary: Hydrothermal veins cutting Precambrian phyllite. Crystallization at ~360 C, 3.5 kbar from metamorphic brines derived from devolatilization of phyllite (Morteani et al. 2002 fluid-inclusion study). The diagnostic imperial color (golden-orange to pink) requires Cr from nearby ultramafic bodies; without Cr the topaz is colorless or pale blue. Single clean cooling curve 360 C -> 50 C; F-pulse mid-scenario gates topaz nucleation.
ouro_preto.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Na, K (phyllite-devolatilization signature: muscovite + biotite + albite breakdown releases alkali cations), Mg (phyllite chlorite/biotite breakdown). No drift between Python and JS init. Existing chemistry preserved (no retuning); 6-event cooling cascade preserved. | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

Na.note: Phyllite albite + muscovite breakdown; metamorphic brine baseline [Morteani 2002]. Na > K typical of phyllite devolatilization.

K.note: Muscovite + biotite breakdown; K/Na ~0.7 (higher than basinal-brine ~0.2 because phyllite micas are K-rich).

Mg.note: Phyllite chlorite + biotite breakdown. Conservative — host is quartzite + phyllite, not mafic. Brief-required non-zero Mg.

preserved_intentional_values.F_note: F=12 is intentionally below topaz saturation threshold (~20 ppm). ev_f_pulse adds +30 from phyllite mica devolatilization to gate topaz nucleation.
preserved_intentional_values.Cr_note: Cr=0.5 is intentionally sub-threshold. ev_cr_leach adds +4 to push into the imperial-color window (3-8 ppm Cr3+ in topaz).
preserved_intentional_values.salinity_note: salinity=3 wt% NaCl-eq is the low-TDS metamorphic-brine signature; consistent with devolatilization-derived fluid (not basinal brine).

references: Morteani, G., Preinfalk, C., Horn, A.H. (2002). Classification and mineralization potential of the pegmatites of the eastern Brazilian pegmatite province. Mineralium Deposita 37: 786-799. [Fluid-inclusion microthermometry for Brazilian topaz / pegmatite hydrothermal veins]
Sauer, J.R. (1982). Brazil: Paradise of Gemstones. Lithografia, Sao Paulo. [Imperial topaz field-relations and historical context]
Pearson, R.S. (1992). The Imperial topaz of Ouro Preto, Brazil. Gems & Gemology 28(2): 88-101. [Color chemistry — Cr3+ origin]
data_gaps: Morteani et al. 2002 covers Brazilian pegmatite-hydrothermal systems broadly; Ouro-Preto-specific LA-ICP-MS fluid-inclusion data is sparse.
Cr source attribution to 'nearby ultramafic dikes' is geologically reasonable but not directly traced in the published literature for individual mines.

intentionally_zero.Au: The region is famously auriferous — 'Ouro Preto' literally means 'Black Gold', and the Iron Quadrangle was the center of the Brazilian gold rush. BUT this scenario simulates the Imperial Topaz veins (Capão / Boa Vista mines) specifically, which are a separate paragenesis from the regional gold-bearing veins (Mariana, Sabará, Nova Lima, Morro Velho). The topaz mines are not gold producers. Au=0 is correct for the topaz-vein-specific scenario, not a gap. A future scenario simulating an Iron Quadrangle gold vein would carry Au.

arsenopyrite.reason: Topaz-quartz pegmatite scenario; As=0 in fluid. Itacolomi formation lacks As affinity.

cruzeiro_doce_valley.display_name: Cruzeiro mine, São José da Safira, Doce Valley, Minas Gerais
cruzeiro_doce_valley.host_formation: Brasiliano-age (Neoproterozoic, 700-450 Ma) granite-pegmatite cutting Macaúbas Group meta-sediments, eastern São Francisco craton margin
cruzeiro_doce_valley.production_history: Type-locality for fine schorl-elbaite tourmaline + smoky quartz + beryl pockets. Active since the early 20th century, still producing world-class specimens.
cruzeiro_doce_valley.fluid_event_summary: Miarolitic gem cavity in the residual pocket of a complex zoned pegmatite. Crystallization 650 -> 300 C across 7 events: outer-shell wall zone, schorl arrival, albitization (K -> Na), Be saturation (beryl), Li phase (spodumene + elbaite), late hydrothermal (topaz), kaolinization (microcline -> kaolinite). Apatite is a documented Cruzeiro accessory mineral [Cassedanne 1991].
cruzeiro_doce_valley.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added P (apatite-enabling, was the gate keeping a documented Cruzeiro accessory from nucleating), Mg (brief-required non-zero baseline, pegmatite-pocket-appropriate low value). No drift between Python and JS init. Existing chemistry (Be, B, Li, F, Cr, Cu, V, Ti, K, Na, Al, etc.) preserved; 7-event cooling cascade preserved. | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

P.note: Pegmatite residual pocket fluids are P-enriched. Apatite (Ca5(PO4)3F) is a documented Cruzeiro accessory; existing Ca=30 + F=25 already supported apatite chemistry — P=0 was the gate. Conservative value — apatite should nucleate as a minor accessory, not dominate the gem signature.

Mg.note: Pegmatite pocket fluids are Mg-poor (Mg partitions strongly into outer-shell biotite/chlorite during pegmatite differentiation). Conservative; brief-required non-zero Mg.

preserved_intentional_values.SiO2_note: Pegmatite-level silica saturation — far above quartz equilibrium at any T, so quartz is supersaturated throughout.
preserved_intentional_values.incompatible_elements_note: Be, B, Li, F all start above their respective minerals' minimum required threshold but below the nucleation σ threshold; they build (unused early) and then the cascade fires as T drops.
preserved_intentional_values.Cu_note: Cu=0.3 is the Paraíba long-shot (1 in 20 scenarios lands the cuprian elbaite given seed variance).

references: Cassedanne, J.P. (1991). Tourmaline-bearing pegmatites in Brazil. Mineralogical Record 22(5): 343-364. [Cruzeiro mine paragenesis, including accessory apatite]
Proctor, K. (1985). Gem pegmatites of Minas Gerais, Brazil: the tourmalines of the Governador Valadares district. Gems & Gemology 21(1): 3-19. [Doce Valley district context]
London, D. (2008). Pegmatites. Canadian Mineralogist Special Publication 10. [Pegmatite fluid chemistry baseline — Al budget, residual-pocket evolution]

Morteani, G., Preinfalk, C., Horn, A.H. (2002). Classification and mineralization potential of the pegmatites of the eastern Brazilian pegmatite province. Mineralium Deposita 37: 786-799. [Fluid-inclusion microthermometry]
data_gaps: P=8 enables apatite nucleation but real Cruzeiro pocket fluid P content is not directly published in fluid-inclusion analyses; conservative estimate.
Cu=0.3 (the Paraíba long-shot) is a deliberately-low value to make cuprian elbaite a rare seed-roll; not a real Cruzeiro Cu measurement.

intentionally_zero.Au: Pegmatite incompatible-element partitioning excludes Au — gold doesn't fit any pegmatite-mineral structure and stays in the residual melt or escapes via late hydrothermal phase. No Brazilian pegmatite is a gold producer, including Cruzeiro. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

arsenopyrite.reason: Gem pegmatite scenario; As=0. Cruzeiro is tourmaline/beryl/spodumene, not As-bearing.

emerald.chemistry_citation: Cr=2.5 ppm in Cruzeiro fluid — from biotite-schist country rock contact (the 'pegmatite-only' emerald paragenesis type, per Giuliani et al. 2019 §3.3). Emerald engine fires first in the beryl-family chromophore priority chain via Cr/V gate. V³⁺ alternative path available (V=2 in Cruzeiro fluid is above the V≥1.0 secondary gate, but Cr wins the chromophore race because it's 100× more efficient at equal ppm).
emerald.reference: Giuliani et al. 2019 (emerald classification); Cassedanne 1991 (Cruzeiro paragenesis)

aquamarine.chemistry_citation: Fe=50 ppm reducing — but aquamarine gate checks Fe ≥ 8 AND NOT (Fe ≥ 15 AND O2 > 0.5). Cruzeiro O2=0.1 (reducing), so aquamarine wins over heliodor. In fluid above Fe=15, only the low O2 keeps it as aquamarine — a classic Cruzeiro/Minas Gerais signature.
aquamarine.reference: Černý 2002 (pegmatite beryl); Adamo et al. 2008 (Fe oxidation)

morganite.chemistry_citation: Mn=8 ppm — well above morganite Mn≥2.0 gate. Morganite late-stage Mn accumulation signature matches Cruzeiro's gem-pocket paragenesis per Cassedanne 1991.

heliodor.reason: Cruzeiro O2=0.1 (reducing) never rises to the heliodor O2>0.5 gate. A different pegmatite with oxidizing pocket fluid (Volodarsk Namibia type) would fire heliodor from similar Fe chemistry.

beryl_goshenite.reason: Cr=2.5, Mn=8, Fe=50 — every chromophore gate is met, so the beryl/goshenite engine is always suppressed by priority exclusion. Goshenite would only fire in a Be-rich pocket that remained chromophore-clean.

notes: Priority chain in effect: emerald > morganite > heliodor > aquamarine > goshenite.
One beryl-family nucleation per step (shared Be pool) — dispatch evaluates candidates in priority order.

Seed-42 gem_pegmatite at SIM_VERSION=7 produces: 4 emerald, 4 aquamarine, 3 morganite (0 heliodor, 0 goshenite). The absence of heliodor/goshenite is a geological truth about this locality, not a bug.

tsumeb.display_name: Tsumeb mine, Otavi Mountain Land, Namibia
tsumeb.host_formation: Pipe-shaped Pb-Zn-Cu sulfide body in Neoproterozoic dolomite (Otavi Group)
tsumeb.production_history: Active 1905-1996. Primary commodities Pb-Zn-Cu-Ag-Ge-Cd. ~280 species documented — among the most mineralogically diverse deposits ever discovered. Type locality for germanium (germanite, renierite, briartite).
tsumeb.fluid_event_summary: 1st-stage gossan = uppermost of three Mesozoic-Cenozoic supergene oxidation zones, ~35 C, oxidizing meteoric brine in dolomitic carbonate cavity. Repeated soil-zone flushes deliver Pb+Mo, As+Co+Ni, P, V in sequential pulses, each unlocking a different mineral assemblage (wulfenite, mimetite/erythrite/annabergite, pyromorphite, vanadinite). Argentiferous throughout; Ge-bearing minerals are the Tsumeb signature.
tsumeb.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Ag (Tsumeb's silver suite: native Ag, proustite, pyrargyrite), Ge (the type-locality element; FluidChemistry.Ge field has carried a 'Tsumeb speciality' comment since the schema was written, this audit finally populates it), Sb (proustite/pyrargyrite/tetrahedrite enabling), Na/K (minor groundwater cation traces). Existing chemistry preserved (Mg=5, V=1.5, P=0.5, etc.); 8-event sequence preserved; Co/Ni-via-event preserved. | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite. | v5-gap-fill (Apr 2026, Tsumeb) — added ev_supergene_acidification event (4 pulses at steps 5/8/12/16) to maintain the early acidic supergene phase against limestone wall buffering. Bumped Al 3 → 25. Bumped jarosite + alunite per-check probability 0.18/0.15 → 0.45. Unlocks scorodite + jarosite + alunite + brochantite in the simulator (4 of 4 BACKLOG'd Tsumeb-pH-gap minerals; antlerite correctly remains absent as Tsumeb is brochantite-dominant per geology).

Ag.note: Tsumeb is one of the most argentiferous deposits ever — native silver, argentiferous galena, proustite, pyrargyrite, stephanite. Existing Cl=20 + new Ag=8 also supports chlorargyrite chemistry [Pinch & Wilson 1977].

Ge.note: Tsumeb is THE type locality for germanium. Germanite (Cu26Fe4Ge4S32), renierite, and briartite were all first described here. FluidChemistry's Ge field has carried a 'Tsumeb speciality' comment since the schema was written — this audit finally populates it [Melcher 2003 Tsumeb Ge geochemistry].

Sb.note: Enables proustite (Ag3SbS3) and pyrargyrite (Ag3SbS3) — the ruby silvers — plus tetrahedrite. Mirrors the Bisbee-style Sb-As-Bi greisen-trace abstraction at lower supergene-zone abundance.

Na.note: Minor groundwater cation trace; supergene meteoric water is dilute (salinity stays at 2 wt%) but carries some Na from soil-zone weathering.

Au.note: Rare native gold IS documented at Tsumeb [Pinch & Wilson 1977] though it's not a primary commodity. Sub-0.5-ppm threshold (grow_native_gold's nucleation cutoff) means no gold crystals nucleate — the 0.3 value documents the reported trace chemistry without producing gold the locality doesn't actually produce in quantity. Added when the Au field landed (post grow_native_gold engine).

Al.note: v5 gap-fill (Apr 2026): two coordinated changes unlock four supergene minerals at Tsumeb. (1) Al bumped 3.0 → 25 to clear the alunite engine's Al/25 cap. (2) New ev_supergene_acidification event scheduled at steps 5, 8, 12, 16 (4 pulses to overrun the limestone wall's pH-buffering) drops pH to 4.0, opening a ~15-step acid window before ev_meteoric_flush (step 20) carbonate-buffers pH back to 6.2. The acid window is when scorodite + jarosite + alunite nucleate. Plus jarosite + alunite per-check probability bumped 0.18/0.15 → 0.45 to reflect their fast acid-sulfate kinetics in brief acid windows. Source: Hemley 1969 + Stoffregen 2000 (alunite/jarosite stability + paragenesis); Pinch & Wilson 1977 (Tsumeb mineralogy).

preserved_intentional_values.Co_note: Co=0 at init by design; delivered by event_as_rich_seep at step 95 (+20). Erythrite (Co arsenate) requires As + Co coincident.
preserved_intentional_values.Ni_note: Ni=0 at init by design; same delivery pattern as Co. Annabergite (Ni arsenate) requires As + Ni coincident.
preserved_intentional_values.P_note: P=0.5 is sub-pyromorphite-threshold by design; ev_phosphate_seep at step 115 adds +6 to gate pyromorphite nucleation.
preserved_intentional_values.V_note: V=1.5 is sub-vanadinite-threshold by design; ev_v_bearing_seep at step 130 adds +6 to gate vanadinite nucleation.
preserved_intentional_values.CO3_note: CO3=110 is just below azurite's high-pCO2 gate (CO3 >= 120); ev_meteoric_flush at step 20 adds +30 to push past threshold.

references: Pinch, W.W., Wilson, W.E. (1977). Minerals of the Tsumeb mine. Mineralogical Record 8(3): 17-37 [special issue]. [Canonical Tsumeb mineralogy and paragenesis monograph]

Lombaard, A.F., Gunzel, A., Innes, J., Kruger, T.L. (1986). The Tsumeb lead-copper-zinc-silver deposit, South West Africa/Namibia. In: Anhaeusser, C.R., Maske, S. (eds.), Mineral Deposits of Southern Africa, Geol. Soc. South Africa, p. 1761-1787. [District geology]

Melcher, F. (2003). The Otavi Mountain Land in Namibia: Tsumeb, germanium and snowball Earth. Mitt. Osterr. Mineral. Gesell. 148: 413-435. [Ge geochemistry — primary source for the Ge=5 gap-fill value]
Hughes, F.E. (1987). Tsumeb (Tsumcorp Mine), Namibia. Econ. Geol. 82(7): 1739-1771. [Three supergene oxidation zones]

Frenzel, M., Hirsch, T., Gutzmer, J. (2016). Gallium, germanium, indium, and other trace and minor elements in sphalerite as a function of deposit type — A meta-analysis. Ore Geology Reviews 76: 52-78. [Modern Ge geochemistry framework]
data_gaps: Ag, Ge, Sb values are Tsumeb-relevant orders-of-magnitude estimates derived from district mineralogy + general supergene-fluid Ag/Ge/Sb ranges; direct fluid-inclusion LA-ICP-MS for Tsumeb supergene fluids is not in the standard literature (most published Ge work is on solid-mineral Ge content, not paleo-fluid Ge content).
The 'three oxidation zones' of Tsumeb (1st/2nd/3rd stage gossan) are collapsed to a single supergene scenario; sub-zone differentiation is out of scope.

arsenopyrite.reason: Tsumeb scenario represents the supergene oxidation zone (O2=1.8). Arsenopyrite requires reducing conditions (O2<0.8) which never occur here. CORRECT — Tsumeb arsenopyrite is the primary parent that already oxidized; this scenario simulates the post-oxidation arsenate suite, not the primary phase.
arsenopyrite.real_locality_evidence: Tsumeb primary arsenopyrite did exist (deeper in the orebody) but the Tsumeb-2 collection-zone the scenario simulates is the supergene-oxidation product zone.

scorodite.reason: After v5 ev_supergene_acidification (steps 5/8/12/16) drops pH to 4.0, scorodite nucleates during the ~15-step acid window before ev_meteoric_flush carbonate-buffers pH back to 6.2. Verified seed-42: 1 scorodite crystal forms (max 15 µm). Across 20 seeds the realization rate is ~95% — Tsumeb scorodite is now part of the simulator's supergene assemblage.
scorodite.audit_action: Resolved by v5 commit. The ev_supergene_acidification event mirrors the geological reality of the early acidic supergene phase before carbonate buffering raised pH to the late-stage 6.8.

ferrimolybdite.reason: Tsumeb has Mo=15, Fe=40, O2=1.8, pH=6.8, T=low -> sigma >5 if T low. Will nucleate readily. Substrate prefers existing molybdenite (none in scene) so wall-nucleation dominant.
ferrimolybdite.real_locality_evidence: Tsumeb molybdates documented; ferrimolybdite is the lower-display-value-but-common Mo-oxidation cousin to wulfenite (which IS reported at Tsumeb).

barite.reason: Ba=0 in Tsumeb fluid; primary mineral suite is dominantly Pb-Zn-Cu-Ge-As-Sb. Barite is reported at Tsumeb but in earlier paragenetic stages not modeled by this scenario.

jarosite.reason: After v5 ev_supergene_acidification, K=10 + Fe=40 + S=70 (post-acid) + O2=1.5 + pH=4.0 → sigma 1.06–2.03 across the 4 acid pulses. Per-check bumped to 0.45 for reliable nucleation. Verified seed-42: 1 jarosite crystal (max 2 µm). 20-seed hit rate ~95%. Tsumeb jarosite was world-class but blocked at pH=6.8 pre-v5.
jarosite.audit_action: Resolved by v5 commit (combined with scorodite + alunite via the same single fix).

alunite.reason: After v5 Al bump (3→25) + ev_supergene_acidification, K=10 + Al=25 + S=70 + O2=1.5 + pH=4.0 → sigma 0.79–1.46 across acid pulses (lower than jarosite due to K/15 cap). Per-check bumped to 0.45. Verified seed-42: 1 alunite crystal (max 2 µm). 20-seed hit rate ~70%. Closes the advanced argillic alteration gap at Tsumeb.
alunite.audit_action: Resolved by v5 commit. Both Al chemistry bump and acid-window event were required.

brochantite.reason: Cu (delivered by ev_cu_enrichment) + S=50+ + O2=1.5–2.2 + pH 4.7–6.5 (the brochantite window). Verified seed-42: 15 active brochantite crystals, max 410 µm — significant supergene Cu sulfate yield. Bisbee/Atacama/Tsumeb brochantite paragenesis now reproduces.

antlerite.reason: Antlerite gate is pH ≤ 4 (gate strict). The v5 acid window holds pH at ~4.7 (limestone wall buffers from 4.0 toward 6); antlerite never sees pH ≤ 4 in the buffered regime. This is geologically correct: Tsumeb is brochantite-dominant for supergene Cu-sulfate, not antlerite-dominant (antlerite is the Atacama/Chuquicamata signature, not Tsumeb's).
antlerite.audit_action: Not a gap — Tsumeb chemistry correctly favors brochantite (which IS active at Tsumeb post-v5).

anhydrite.reason: T=35, salinity=2 → low-T dilute branch returns 0 (gypsum/selenite is the appropriate phase). Selenite already in scenario.

deccan_nashik.display_name: Deccan Traps zeolite vesicle, Nashik / Aurangabad area, Maharashtra, India
deccan_nashik.host_formation: Late-Cretaceous Deccan basalt flow vesicles (~66 Ma flood basalt province)
deccan_nashik.fluid_event_summary: Stage III apophyllite-bearing vesicles (~21-58 Ma post-eruption per Ottens et al. 2019). Alkaline K-Ca-Si-F groundwater percolating through cooled basalt vesicles, crystallizing pseudo-cubic apophyllite blocks, sometimes carrying hematite-needle phantom inclusions (the 'bloody apophyllite' of Nashik). 5-stage event sequence: silica veneer (Stage I) -> hematite seeds -> zeolites + calcite (Stage II) -> apophyllite (Stage III) -> late cooling.
deccan_nashik.audit_pass: gap-fill (Apr 2026) — added Sr=2. Deccan zeolites (heulandite, stilbite, mesolite) carry Sr substituting for Ca, sometimes 100s of ppm in the mineral; documenting the parent-fluid source. Existing Mg=8 already meets brief-required non-zero Mg. Drift check: Python and JS init values identical, no fixes needed. Existing chemistry preserved. | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

Sr.note: Deccan zeolites carry Sr substituting for Ca, particularly in heulandite-stilbite series. Sim-scale 2 ppm documents the parent-fluid Sr without nucleating distinct Sr minerals (which is geologically correct — strontianite is not a Deccan vesicle phase).

preserved_intentional_values.K_note: K=2 is intentionally low at init; ev_zeolite_stage_ii (+10) and ev_apophyllite_stage_iii (+25) gate the K-bearing apophyllite/stilbite stage.
preserved_intentional_values.F_note: F=1 is intentionally sub-apophyllite-threshold; ev_apophyllite_stage_iii adds +4 to gate apophyllite nucleation.

references: Ottens, B., Götze, J., Schuster, R., Krenn, K., Hauzenberger, C., Zsolt, B., Weiyang, P. (2019). Exceptional multi stage mineralization of secondary minerals in cavities of flood basalts from the Deccan Volcanic Province, India. Minerals 9(6): 351. [Stage I/II/III fluid evolution model — primary source for the scenario]

Sukheswala, R.N., Avasia, R.K., Gangopadhyay, M. (1974). Zeolites and associated secondary minerals in the Deccan Traps of western India. Mineral. Mag. 39: 658-671. [Classic Deccan zeolite mineralogy]

Pe-Piper, G., Tsolis-Katagas, P. (1991). K-rich mordenite from late Miocene rhyolitic tuffs, Polyegos Island, Greece (and Sr/Ba in zeolites). Clays and Clay Minerals 39: 239-247. [Sr/Ba substitution in zeolite framework — basis for the Sr=2 gap-fill]
data_gaps: Sr=2 is a sim-scale documentation value; direct fluid-inclusion Sr quantification for Deccan vesicle waters is not widely published.
Stage I/II/III timing in the scenario (events at steps 20/35/70/110/160) is a simplification of the much longer ~21-58 Ma real timeline.

intentionally_zero.Au: Deccan basalts are not Au-bearing. Surface-T alkaline groundwater percolating through cooled basalt vesicles doesn't transport Au — wrong T, wrong complex chemistry. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

arsenopyrite.reason: Zeolite scenario in basalt vesicles; As=0. Deccan basalt vesicle minerals are zeolites + apophyllite, not As-bearing.

scorodite.reason: No As; pH=8.2 alkaline (way above scorodite stability).

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo. Zeolite-stage fluid lacks porphyry-Mo affinity.

barite.reason: Zeolite-stage basalt vesicle chemistry; no Ba.

jarosite.reason: pH=8.2 alkaline — opposite end of stability range.

anhydrite.reason: Low Ca + low salinity; selenite-territory if anything.

coorong_sabkha.display_name: Coorong lagoon system, South Australia (and Persian Gulf sabkhas)
coorong_sabkha.host_formation: Modern lagoonal carbonate sediment + evaporite host
coorong_sabkha.fluid_event_summary: Surface-T evaporative brine cycling (~25 C, ~120 wt% NaCl-eq sabkha endmember), 12 flood/evap pulses over 240 steps drive Omega across the dolomite saturation boundary repeatedly. Per Kim, Sun et al. (2023, Science 382:915), this cyclic Omega modulation is what's needed to produce ORDERED dolomite at ambient T (the geological prize the paper made accessible). f_ord reaches >0.7 by mid-scenario.
coorong_sabkha.audit_pass: consistency check (Apr 2026) — no gap-fills needed. The scenario was already exceptionally well-tuned for its specific story. All major sabkha-brine elements present (Mg=1400, Ca=400, CO3=300, Na=10500, K=380, S=2700, Cl=18000, Sr=20, F=2). Drift check: Python and JS init values identical. No changes made. | v3-expansion (Apr 2026) — added arsenopyrite + scorodite + ferrimolybdite engine activations. No fluid chemistry changed; per-locality mineral_realizations_v3_expansion block added documenting expected behavior given existing chemistry. One documented gap: tsumeb scorodite is geologically real but blocked by current pH=6.8 (would need early-acidic-phase event to unlock). | v4-sulfate-expansion (Apr 2026) — added barite + celestine + jarosite + alunite + brochantite + antlerite + anhydrite engines (Round 5). Per-locality mineral_realizations_v4_sulfate_expansion block added documenting expected behavior. SIM_VERSION 3→4 bump driven by Coorong sabkha celestine + anhydrite activation (textbook sabkha-evaporite assemblage now models). Two documented gap families: (1) Tri-State + Sweetwater O2=0.0 blocks barite + celestine despite Ba/Sr present — bumping to mildly-reducing O2=0.2 would unlock both; (2) Tsumeb pH=6.8 blocks jarosite alongside the existing scorodite pH gap — same fix (early event_supergene_acidification) would unlock all three plus alunite.

preserved_intentional_values.Mg_note: Mg=1400 is intentionally high — sabkha endmember is ~3x seawater Mg. Mg/Ca = 3.5 is well above the dolomite-favoring threshold of 1.0.
preserved_intentional_values.salinity_note: salinity=120 wt% NaCl-eq is the sabkha endmember; 12 flood events reset to marine baseline (Mg=800, Ca=250) to drive the cycling.
preserved_intentional_values.Sr_note: Sr=20 is the marine-evaporite tracer; aragonite scavenges Sr during the scenario.

references: Kim, J.L., Sun, Y., Konhauser, K.O., Whitfield, R.E., Vlahos, R., Pasek, M.A. (2023). Solution chemistry control on the formation of dolomite. Science 382(6678): 915-919. [The cyclic-Omega ordered-dolomite mechanism — the scenario's defining citation]
Warren, J. (2000). Dolomite: occurrence, evolution and economically important associations. Earth-Science Reviews 52: 1-81. [Sabkha dolomitization context]
von der Borch, C.C. (1976). Stratigraphy and formation of Holocene dolomitic carbonate deposits of the Coorong area, South Australia. J. Sedim. Petrol. 46: 952-966. [Coorong-specific geology]
data_gaps: None for the declared mineralogy. The scenario covers all relevant elements at realistic concentrations.

intentionally_zero.Au: Surface evaporite from seawater concentration. No Au pathway — seawater Au is sub-ppb, evaporative concentration up to 100x still leaves Au at sub-ppm levels. Au=0 is correct, not a gap.

arsenopyrite.reason: Evaporite/dolomitization scenario, low-T (<50 C). As=0; no sulfide chemistry.

ferrimolybdite.reason: No Mo. Coorong sabkha is dolomite-gypsum-halite, no molybdate signature.

barite.reason: Ba=0 in Coorong sabkha (correctly absent — sabkha is Sr-dominant, not Ba).

celestine.reason: Sr=20 ✓, S=2700 (massive) ✓, O2=1.5 ✓, T=25 + low T-branch fires through the Sr-celestine path. **THIS IS THE SEED-42 SHIFT THAT JUSTIFIED SIM_VERSION 3→4 BUMP.** Verified: 18 celestine crystals form across the run.
celestine.real_locality_evidence: Modern Persian Gulf + Coorong + Salar de Atacama sabkhas all produce celestine alongside dolomite + halite + gypsum/anhydrite stratigraphy. Madagascar Sakoany geodes are the world-class display source.

anhydrite.reason: T=25 + salinity=120 (above 100‰ threshold) + Ca=400 + S=2700 + O2=1.5. Sigma=7.5; verified seed-42: 8 anhydrite crystals up to 952 µm. Sabkha now produces the textbook gypsum + anhydrite + celestine + dolomite + aragonite assemblage. The low-T saline-branch anhydrite paragenesis (Persian Gulf sabkha analog).
anhydrite.real_locality_evidence: Persian Gulf sabkha + Coorong + Salar de Atacama all preserve interlayered anhydrite-gypsum-halite stratigraphy. Hardie 1967 phase diagram is the canonical reference.

mogok_stone_tract.display_name: Mogok Stone Tract, Mandalay Region, Burma (Myanmar)
mogok_stone_tract.host_formation: Mogok Metamorphic Belt — Cambro-Ordovician dolomitic marble regionally metamorphosed at amphibolite-to-granulite facies during the Himalayan orogeny (~30 Ma), then intruded by leucogranite dykes at 17-22 Ma. The contact-metamorphic aureole between leucogranite and marble is the corundum (ruby/sapphire/spinel) paragenesis host.
mogok_stone_tract.production_history: World's source of finest ruby for ~2000+ years. Mogok rubies have been exported since at least the 6th century; Mughal and Chinese imperial courts both prized them. 19th-century British colonial period saw industrialized extraction; modern production continues through the Burma state enterprise + small-scale mining. Mong Hsu district (1990s-discovered) expanded the Mogok-type paragenesis.
mogok_stone_tract.fluid_event_summary: T profile 500 → 700 (peak) → 500 → 350°C over 180 steps. Peak metamorphism event at step 20 pumps fluid + bumps Cr +1.5 (additional from ultramafic serpentinite contact). Retrograde cooling at step 60. Fracture seal at step 150.

intentionally_zero.B: marble-hosted fluids carry no boron (no pegmatite contribution). B=0 is the correct baseline.
intentionally_zero.Be: no beryllium source in dolomitic marble; beryl doesn't form here.
intentionally_zero.Li: metamorphic fluid — lithium depleted relative to pegmatite fluids.
intentionally_zero.S: non-sulfide regime; Mogok rubies don't coexist with sulfides.

ruby.seed_42_hit_rate: 100% (1 crystal nucleated at seed-42, max ~62 µm in 180 steps)
ruby.chemistry_citation: Cr=3 ppm + 1.5 bump from peak_metamorphism event = 4.5 peak Cr. Well above ruby Cr≥2.0 gate. Takes priority over sapphire in the corundum-family chromophore priority chain — sapphire gate requires Fe ≥ 5 but is suppressed by Cr ≥ 2 until Cr depletes. Seed-42 run stays Cr-dominant throughout the growth window; sapphire does not fire. Color tag fires 'Mogok ruby red' at Cr 3-5 ppm; 'pigeon's blood' tag requires Cr > 5 AND Fe > 1 — achievable in the 700°C peak pulse.
ruby.reference: Garnier et al. 2008 (marble-hosted ruby); Peretti et al. 2018

sapphire.reason: Cr=3 (and event bump +1.5) holds above ruby gate throughout the 180-step window; sapphire priority exclusion prevents nucleation. Sapphire-blue gate (Fe ≥ 5 AND Ti ≥ 0.5) IS met by the fluid (Fe=8, Ti=1), but ruby takes precedence.
sapphire.future_work: To get sapphire nucleation visible in seed-42, one option is a scenario variant where Cr is locally depleted (e.g., split pocket where Cr arrives earlier and rubies deplete it before sapphire nucleation window opens). Alternatively, add a Kashmir-type sapphire scenario with Cr=0 (the real Kashmir paragenesis is Cr-poor).

corundum.reason: Both Cr >= 2 (ruby) and Fe >= 5 (sapphire) conditions are met; colorless corundum has no path to fire. Corundum (colorless) fires only in Cr-poor AND Fe-poor marble contact fluids.

notes: Marble-hosted corundum paragenesis: the Mogok Stone Tract is the world's type locality. Gem ruby since at least 6th century CE.
Phlogopite, spinel, chondrodite, graphite, and rutile (as needle inclusions) are the typical Mogok accessory minerals — future scenario iterations could add them for paragenetic completeness.
Calcite + aragonite also nucleate at seed-42 (from Ca=800, CO3=50) — these are the 'marble' part of the marble-hosted ruby paragenesis; not a bug.

references: Garnier, V. et al. 2008. 'Marble-hosted ruby deposits from Central and Southeast Asia: Towards a new genetic model.' Ore Geology Reviews 34: 169-191.
Peretti, A. et al. 2018. 'Update on corundum and its gem varieties.' Gems & Gemology special issue.
Searle, M.P. et al. 2007. 'Tectonic evolution of the Mogok metamorphic belt, Burma (Myanmar) constrained by U-Th-Pb dating of metamorphic and magmatic rocks.' Journal of Geology 115: 1-23.
Themelis, T. 2008. Gems & Mines of Mogok. Yangon: A&T Publishing. [Comprehensive Mogok mining history]
mogok_stone_tract.audit_status: Round 7 new locality. Fluid chemistry cross-referenced with scenario_marble_contact_metamorphism in vugg.py + web/index.html. Pending schema additions: none.
